# @express-route-cache

> Production-grade, drop-in route caching for Express.js with O(1) invalidation, SWR, and Stampede Protection.

## Features & Core Logic

- **O(1) Epoch Invalidation**: Uses versioned keys (v:pattern=N) to instantly invalidate route trees via atomic `INCR` commands. Zero key scanning.
- **Sequential Integrity**: All invalidations (via `autoInvalidate` or `cache.invalidate()`) are tied to the `res.on('finish')` event to ensure the DB update completes before the cache is cleared.
- **Binary Support**: Automatically serializes non-string responses (Buffers, Images, PDFs) to Base64 to prevent UTF-8 corruption.
- **Request Coalescing**: Stampede protection keeps only 1 active handler in-flight for any unique cache-miss key.

## Monorepo Modules

- `packages/core`: Core middleware and internal logic.
- `packages/adapter-redis`: Redis (ioredis) backend.
- `packages/adapter-memcached`: Memcached (memjs) backend.

## Guidance for AI Agents

- **Invalidation**: Always use `res.on('finish')` for cleanup or invalidation to avoid race conditions.
- **Binary Data**: Use the `isBase64` flag in `CacheEntry` metadata when working with raw Buffers.
- **Headers**: Use `res.getHeaders()` to capture full response state for replay.
- **Async Logic**: Caching and background revalidation (SWR) are fire-and-forget; do not block the main response cycle.

## Documentation References

- [Main README](README.md)
- [Architecture & Trade-offs](ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [API Reference](packages/core/README.md)
